revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=台東区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]2-24-…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=台東区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]2-24-1 |地図 = |祭神 = [[大物主]]（[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 竹町金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]]～[[1615年]]）に創建された。創建者の[[生駒親正]]は[[高松藩]]の[[藩主]]であり、同じ[[讃岐国]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]の分霊を[[生駒氏|生駒家]]の[[江戸藩邸|中屋敷]]に勧請することになった。これが当社の起源である<ref name="下谷">東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年、956-957p</ref><ref name="東京神社">東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年、102-103p</ref>。
[[江戸時代]]、一般人が大名屋敷に立ち入ることは禁止されていた。しかし金刀比羅大神は一般人にも人気のある神であるので、参拝を希望する者が相次いでいた。そこで生駒家では[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に願い出て、特例として毎月10日の参拝が認められることになった<ref name="下谷"></ref>。
後に生駒家は[[お家騒動]]（[[生駒騒動]]）を起こし、最終的に[[大名]]から[[交代寄合]][[旗本]]に降格になった。しかし江戸の中屋敷はそのまま生駒家の屋敷として存続し、金刀比羅神社の祭祀も滞ることなく続けてきた<ref name="下谷"></ref>。
[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）の[[関東大震災]]や[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[東京大空襲]]で、その都度焼失している。現在の社殿は、[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に新築されたものである<ref name="下谷"></ref><ref name="東京神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[御徒町駅]]または[[仲御徒町駅]]より徒歩3分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年 *東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや たいとうく}} [[Category:台東区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

[[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）の[[関東大震災]]や[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[東京大空襲]]で、その都度焼失している。現在の社殿は、[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に新築されたものである<ref name="下谷"></ref><ref name="東京神社"></ref>。

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]2-24-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[大物主]]（[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 竹町金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]の[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に創建された。創建者の[[生駒親正]]は[[高松藩]]の[[藩主]]であり、同じ[[讃岐国]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]の分霊を[[生駒氏|生駒家]]の[[江戸藩邸|中屋敷]]に勧請することになった。これが当社の起源である東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年、956-957p東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年、102-103p。 [[江戸時代]]、一般人が大名屋敷に立ち入ることは禁止されていた。しかし金刀比羅大神は一般人にも人気のある神であるので、参拝を希望する者が相次いでいた。そこで生駒家では[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に願い出て、特例として毎月10日の参拝が認められることになった。 後に生駒家は[[お家騒動]]（[[生駒騒動]]）を起こし、最終的に[[大名]]から[[交代寄合]][[旗本]]に降格になった。しかし江戸の中屋敷はそのまま生駒家の屋敷として存続し、金刀比羅神社の祭祀も滞ることなく続けてきた。 [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）の[[関東大震災]]や[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[東京大空襲]]で、その都度焼失している。現在の社殿は、[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に新築されたものである。 == 交通アクセス == *[[御徒町駅]]または[[仲御徒町駅]]より徒歩3分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年 *東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年 {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:台東区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:下谷]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shitaya]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]]

Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shitaya]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shitaya]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link incomplete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | AlternateName = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Konpira Shrine {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:Unsorted completion {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]]

{{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Taito)-qq]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Taito)-qq]] This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Taito)-qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Taito)-qq]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References ==
