{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year }}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year }}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year }}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year }}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year }} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year }}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year }} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year }}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year }} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year }}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year }}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|12=draft}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|12=draft}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|12=draft}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|12=draft}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|12=draft}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|12=draft}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|12=draft}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|12=draft}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|12=draft}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|12=draft}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|12=draft}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=draft}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|12=draft}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|12=draft}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year|12=draft}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|12=draft}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]]

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区) up to rev 101929342
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
社殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[台東区]][[台東 (台東区)|台東]]2-24-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} |地図 = |祭神 = [[大物主]]（[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]） |社格 = |創建 = [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = 竹町金刀比羅神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[台東区]]の[[神社]]。 == 歴史 == [[慶長]]年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に創建された。創建者の[[生駒親正]]は[[高松藩]]の[[藩主]]であり、同じ[[讃岐国]]にある[[金刀比羅宮]]の[[金毘羅権現|金刀比羅大神]]の分霊を[[生駒氏|生駒家]]の[[江戸藩邸|中屋敷]]に勧請することになった。これが当社の起源である東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年、956-957p東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年、102-103p。 [[江戸時代]]、一般人が大名屋敷に立ち入ることは禁止されていた。しかし金刀比羅大神は一般人にも人気のある神であるので、参拝を希望する者が相次いでいた。そこで生駒家では[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に願い出て、特例として毎月10日の参拝が認められることになった。 後に生駒家は[[お家騒動]]（[[生駒騒動]]）を起こし、最終的に[[大名]]から[[交代寄合]][[旗本]]に降格になった。しかし江戸の中屋敷はそのまま生駒家の屋敷として存続し、金刀比羅神社の祭祀も滞ることなく続けてきた。 [[1923年]]（[[大正]]12年）の[[関東大震災]]や[[1945年]]（[[昭和]]20年）の[[東京大空襲]]で、その都度焼失している。現在の社殿は、[[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）に新築されたものである。 == 交通アクセス == *[[御徒町駅]]または[[仲御徒町駅]]より徒歩3分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *東京市下谷区 編『下谷区史』東京市下谷区、1935年 *東京都神社庁 編『東京都神社名鑑 上巻』東京都神社庁、1986年 {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや}} [[Category:台東区の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:下谷]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] to [[Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}}

However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|12=draft}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]]

However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito (Taito City)|qq=}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikamasa|qq=}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raids|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}（{{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}}） |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type= |創建={{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}. == History == It was founded during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}（{{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}}） |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type= |創建={{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}. == History == It was founded during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito (Taito City)|qq=}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikamasa|qq=}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raids|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Shitaya]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]

Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed.
