Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito (Taito City)|qq=}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikamasa|qq=}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raids|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}（{{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}}） |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type= |創建={{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}. == History == It was founded during the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}（{{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}}） |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type= |創建={{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}. == History == It was founded during the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito (Taito City)|qq=}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikamasa|qq=}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raids|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]

Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, {{ill|Taito (Taito City)|ja|台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito (Taito City)|qq=}} 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ({{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}}) | Classification = | Established = {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Taito City|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito City|qq=}}, Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} – {{ill|1615|ja|1615年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikamasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikamasa|qq=}}, the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}}, enshrined a branch of {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} from {{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}} to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an {{ill|Oie Sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oie Sōdō|qq=}} ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the {{ill|Tokyo air raids|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raids|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka-Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}（{{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}}） |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |type= |創建={{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation={{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}{{ill|Taito Ward|ja|台東区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito Ward|qq=}}. == History == It was founded during the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, {{ill|Ikoma Chikemasa|ja|生駒親正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Chikemasa|qq=}}, was the lord of the {{ill|Takamatsu Domain|ja|高松藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsu Domain|qq=}} and, in the same {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki Province|qq=}}, the sub-spirit of {{ill|Kotohira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|lt=Kotohira Great Deity|lt_ja=金刀比羅大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Gongen|qq=}} was enshrined at the {{ill|Ikoma Family|ja|生駒氏|lt=Ikoma Family|lt_ja=生駒家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Family|qq=}}'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as {{ill|Ouchi-sōdō|ja|お家騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouchi-sōdō|qq=}}), and ultimately was demoted from {{ill|daimyō|ja|大名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/daimyō|qq=}} to {{ill|hatamoto|ja|旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hatamoto|qq=}} through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era) and the {{ill|Tokyo air raid|ja|東京大空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo air raid|qq=}} of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either {{ill|Okachimachi Station|ja|御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okachimachi Station|qq=}} or {{ill|Naka‑Okachimachi Station|ja|仲御徒町駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka‑Okachimachi Station|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], [[:en:台東|Taito (Taito City)]] 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ([[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity=[[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]（[[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]]） |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type= |創建=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]. == History == It was founded during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikemasa]], was the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]] and, in the same [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], the sub-spirit of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]] was enshrined at the [[:en:Ikoma clan|Ikoma Family]]'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Ouchi-sōdō]]), and ultimately was demoted from [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] to [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], [[:en:台東|Taito (Taito City)]] 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ([[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity=[[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]（[[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]]） |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type= |創建=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]. == History == It was founded during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikemasa]], was the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]] and, in the same [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], the sub-spirit of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]] was enshrined at the [[:en:Ikoma clan|Ikoma Family]]'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Ouchi-sōdō]]), and ultimately was demoted from [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] to [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:下谷]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity=[[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]（[[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]]） |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type= |創建=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]. == History == It was founded during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=}}). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikemasa]], was the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]] and, in the same [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], the sub-spirit of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]] was enshrined at the [[:en:Ikoma clan|Ikoma Family]]'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Ouchi-sōdō]]), and ultimately was demoted from [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] to [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], [[:en:台東|Taito (Taito City)]] 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ([[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]

It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito, Tokyo]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], [[:en:台東|Taito (Taito City)]] 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ([[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}} and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1963年}}. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity=[[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]（[[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]]） |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type= |創建=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]. == History == It was founded during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikemasa]], was the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]] and, in the same [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], the sub-spirit of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]] was enshrined at the [[:en:Ikoma clan|Ikoma Family]]'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Ouchi-sōdō]]), and ultimately was demoted from [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] to [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito, Tokyo]]

Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja|大正12年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja|昭和20年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja|昭和38年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1963年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]

However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], [[:en:台東|Taito (Taito City)]] 2-24-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=金刀比羅神社 (台東区)}} | Map = | Deities = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) ([[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]) | MainHall = | Alternatename = Takechō Kotohira Shrine | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Taitō|Taito City]], Tokyo. == History == The shrine was established during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ([[:en:1596|1596]] – [[:en:1615|1615]]). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikamasa]], the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]], enshrined a branch of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] to the Ikoma clan’s {{ill|Edo residence|ja|江戸藩邸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo residence|qq=}}. This marks the origin of the shrine.Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935, pp. 956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986, pp. 102–103 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], public access to daimyō residences was prohibited. However, due to the popularity of Konpira Gongen, many desired to visit. The Ikoma family sought permission from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a special exemption allowed public worship on the 10th day of every month. Later, the Ikoma clan faced an [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Oie Sōdō]] ({{ill|Ikoma Sōdō|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma Sōdō|qq=}}), which led to their demotion from daimyō to {{ill|Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|ja|交代寄合旗本|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai-yoriai hatamoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, their Edo residence remained, and the shrine continued its rituals uninterrupted. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|qq=}} in {{ill|1923|ja||1923年|1=1923|2=ja|3=1923年|lt=Taisho 11|lt_ja=大正11年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1923年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] and again in the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raids]] of {{ill|1945|ja||1945年|1=1945|2=ja|3=1945年|lt=Showa 20|lt_ja=昭和20年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1945年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. The current hall was reconstructed in {{ill|1963|ja||1963年|1=1963|2=ja|3=1963年|lt=Showa 38|lt_ja=昭和38年||qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1963年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]]. == Access == * A 3-minute walk from [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka-Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, ''Shitaya Ward History'', Tokyo City Shitaya Ward, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, ''Tokyo Shrine Directory, Volume 1'', Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Bureau, 1986 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] }} [[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[sn:onpira Shrine (Taito)]] ==Merged second translation== {{神社 ||name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区)) |native_name=金刀比羅神社 |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image=[[File:金刀比羅神社 (台東区).jpg|250px]]
Shrine Building
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E}}}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]{{ill|Taito|ja|台東 (台東区)|lt=Taito|lt_ja=台東|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taito|qq=}}2-24-1 |location={{coordinates|35|42|12.1|N|139|46|42.6|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社 (台東区))}} |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |deity=[[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]]（[[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]]） |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |type= |創建=[[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |本殿= |別名=Takecho Kotohira Shrine (竹町金刀比羅神社) |札所等= |religious_affiliation=[[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |festival= |神事= }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]][[:en:Taitō|Taito Ward]]. == History == It was founded during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] period ({{ill|1596|ja|1596|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1596|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1000}} - {{ill|1615|ja|1615|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1615|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Its founder, [[:en:Ikoma Chikamasa|Ikoma Chikemasa]], was the lord of the [[:en:Takamatsu Domain|Takamatsu Domain]] and, in the same [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], the sub-spirit of [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Kotohira Great Deity]] was enshrined at the [[:en:Ikoma clan|Ikoma Family]]'s {{ill|Edo Residence|ja|江戸藩邸|lt=Edo Residence|lt_ja=中屋敷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Residence|qq=}}. This is the origin of this shrine.Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935, pp.956–957Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], ordinary people were prohibited from entering daimyō residences. However, since Kotohira Great Deity was popular even among commoners, many wished to worship him. Consequently, the Ikoma family petitioned the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|幕府|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|lt_ja=幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and as an exception, worship on the 10th day of every month was permitted. Later, the Ikoma family was involved in a family feud (the {{ill|Ikoma disturbance|ja|生駒騒動|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikoma disturbance|qq=}}, also known as [[:en:O-Ie Sōdō|Ouchi-sōdō]]), and ultimately was demoted from [[:en:Daimyo|daimyō]] to [[:en:Hatamoto|hatamoto]] through a system of alternating assemblies. However, the Edo residence continued to exist as the residence of the Ikoma family, and the rituals of Kotohira Shrine continued uninterrupted. It was destroyed by fire each time during the {{ill|Kantō earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō earthquake|qq=}} of {{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (12th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era) and the [[:en:Bombing of Tokyo|Tokyo air raid]] of {{ill|1945|ja|1945|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1945|qq=}} (20th year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era). The current shrine building was newly constructed in {{ill|1963|ja|1963|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1963|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (38th year of the Shōwa era). == Access == * A 3‑minute walk from either [[:en:Okachimachi Station|Okachimachi Station]] or [[:en:Naka-okachimachi Station|Naka‑Okachimachi Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Tokyo City Shimotani District, ed. "History of Shimotani District", Tokyo City Shimotani District, 1935 * Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, ed. "Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Directory, Volume 1", Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency, 1986, pp.102–103 {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|金刀比羅神社 (台東区)|version=101929342|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Taito Ward]] [[Category:Kotohira Shrines]] [[Category:Shimotani]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Kotohira Shrine (Taito Ward)]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (台東区)]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Taito)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shitaya]] [[Category:Shrines in Taito, Tokyo]]
